intro: Oxymeris cornioides, also known as Chinese Cornelian cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxymeris cornioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, and its extracts are also used to make capsules, tablets, and tonics.
